text: The surgeon uses an extra five millimeter port in the patient's left upper quadrant for additional manipulation of the distended gallbladder. Due to the large size of the stones, a medial-first dissection approach is employed, described as 'opening up the book.' The gallbladder is being mobilized off the cystic plate laterally, indicating the need for an extraction incision.

The surgeon completes the mobilization of the gallbladder, dissecting it off the liver from medial to lateral. The cystic artery is clipped and transected, preparing for the gallbladder's externalization through a mini laparotomy.
